description: Enriches the Astronomy API with AI-optimized descriptions, usage hints, and examples that help LLMs and AI agents understand and correctly invoke each operation.

info.description: REST API for querying astronomical objects including stars, constellations, and planets. Designed for educational tools, planetarium software, and astronomy research applications. All coordinates use the J2000 epoch unless otherwise noted.

data.description: Returns full details of a constellation by its three-letter IAU abbreviation (e.g. 'Ori' for Orion, 'UMa' for Ursa Major). Use this when you know the standard abbreviation. For name-based lookup, use searchConstellations instead.

x-ai-hints.typicalUsage: Resolving a known constellation abbreviation to its full metadata including boundaries and brightest stars.

data.description: Search constellations by partial name, mythology origin, or visibility from a given latitude. Returns a paginated list. Use 'fields' parameter to limit response size when you only need names and abbreviations.

x-ai-hints.typicalUsage: Finding constellations when the user provides a partial or colloquial name rather than the official IAU abbreviation.

data.description: Retrieve a star by its catalog identifier (Hipparcos, Henry Draper, or Bayer designation). The catalogType parameter selects which catalog system to query.

x-ai-hints.typicalUsage: Looking up a specific star when you have its catalog reference from a scientific source.
